# UC-15.3.12 · Fire Suppression System Status

## Description

Pre-action system armed/disarmed, agent levels (FM-200/Novec) ensure fire suppression readiness; disarmed or low-agent systems leave the data center unprotected.

## Value

Pre-action system armed/disarmed, agent levels (FM-200/Novec) ensure fire suppression readiness; disarmed or low-agent systems leave the data center unprotected.

## Implementation

Integrate fire suppression panel via BMS, SNMP, or vendor API. Ingest system_armed (armed/disarmed), agent_level_pct, alarm_active. Poll every 15–30 minutes. Alert immediately on disarmed state, low agent (<95%), or active alarm. Track inspection dates. Report on system readiness for compliance.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom (BMS integration, SNMP).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Fire suppression panel telemetry (system_armed, agent_level_pct, alarm_active).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Integrate fire suppression panel via BMS, SNMP, or vendor API. Ingest system_armed (armed/disarmed), agent_level_pct, alarm_active. Poll every 15–30 minutes. Alert immediately on disarmed state, low agent (<95%), or active alarm. Track inspection dates. Report on system readiness for compliance.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="fire:suppression_status"
| where system_armed!="armed" OR agent_level_pct < 95 OR alarm_active="true"
| table _time, zone, system_armed, agent_level_pct, alarm_active, last_inspection_date
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Fire Suppression System Status** — Pre-action system armed/disarmed, agent levels (FM-200/Novec) ensure fire suppression readiness; disarmed or low-agent systems leave the data center unprotected.

Documented **Data sources**: Fire suppression panel telemetry (system_armed, agent_level_pct, alarm_active). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom (BMS integration, SNMP). **Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="fire:suppression_status". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where system_armed!="armed" OR agent_level_pct < 95 OR alarm_active="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Fire Suppression System Status**): table _time, zone, system_armed, agent_level_pct, alarm_active, last_inspection_date
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Compare with the BMS console for the same equipment, sensors, and time range.

## SPL

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="fire:suppression_status"
| where system_armed!="armed" OR agent_level_pct < 95 OR alarm_active="true"
| table _time, zone, system_armed, agent_level_pct, alarm_active, last_inspection_date
| sort -_time
```

## Visualization

Status grid (zone × armed/agent status), Table (zones needing attention), Gauge (agent level %), Single value (systems disarmed).
